headline: Long-running agent architecture broke out this week, led by separated evaluation and subagents.
body: Separated evaluation and subagents both broke out as the structural patterns behind multi-day agents, with Google Cloud Tech laying out event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation as the three core designs for workflows that outlast stateless chatbots. Persistent memory reinforced the theme: Hermes tools for Claude maintain a unified memory store and skill library across sessions, and the open-source Hermes Agent adds cron scheduling and self-improving AI that writes its own skills. On the enterprise side, Anthropic shipped enterprise-managed authentication for MCP connectors, moving agent deployment toward identity-provider control. New technique entrants including delegation chains, distillation, latent caching, and robot policy signal where the next rotation is forming.

cited_items.title: Hermes Agent Tutorial: How to Setup & Use Open Source AI Agent for Beginners
3 patterns to build long-running AI agents
